problem.statement: Marketing campaigns, news tickers and partner-submitted offers require one governed DSH lifecycle with explicit targeting, scheduling, review, client visibility, measurement and multi-surface readback.
problem.currentWorkaround: The repository contains separate campaign, ticker and partner-offer capabilities, but campaign scheduling fields are not fully mutable, offer transitions are not explicitly constrained, and the client surface only sees manually authored home content rather than the canonical approved offer projection.

permittedActions: pause complete cancel and archive campaigns
create publish pause and archive tickers
review approve reject pause and archive partner offers
read canonical impressions clicks and audit evidence
forbiddenActions: publish a rejected or expired offer
calculate or persist WLT financial truth

actors.role: Owner of a client-visible store submitting a commercial offer
permittedActions: submit an offer for the authenticated owned store
list and read offers for the owned store
read rejection reason and canonical review state
forbiddenActions: submit an offer for another store
bypass coupon or store visibility gates

actors.role: Viewer of eligible active campaigns tickers and partner offers
permittedActions: open governed campaign and offer targets
emit deduplicated impression and click events
forbiddenActions: read draft paused rejected archived expired or ineligible marketing content
mutate campaign ticker or partner-offer truth
submit another actor identity in measurement events

routesOrScreens: owned partner-offer list and submission form

surfaces.exclusionReason: JRN-025 registry scope is operator, partner and client; captain operational notifications remain owned by JRN-023.
Field readiness and store quality remain owned by JRN-024 and do not own campaign or partner-offer publication truth.

outcome.statement: Every campaign ticker and partner offer has one auditable DSH lifecycle, and only currently eligible approved content is projected to the client with measurable impressions and clicks.
outcome.baselineState: Core entities and operator routes exist, but scheduling mutation, strict offer transitions and canonical client offer projection are incomplete.
outcome.targetState: Operator, partner and client surfaces consume the same versioned marketing truth and eligible home-discovery projection with audit and measurement evidence.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted static, database, runtime, accessibility and independent product review

criteria: Campaign schedule audience placement and target are mutable through the governed operator API.
Campaign and partner-offer writes reject stale expected versions.
Campaign and partner-offer lifecycle transitions are explicit and fail closed.
Activation or publication revalidates target visibility and schedule eligibility.
Partner submission is always bound to the authenticated owned store.
Rejection requires a non-empty reason and archive is soft and terminal.
Home discovery projects only active in-window client-eligible campaigns and published in-window partner offers.
Client impressions and clicks are accepted only for currently publishable projected content.
Operator and partner mutations refresh canonical API readback.
No local mock or static array represents runtime campaign or offer truth.
requiredChecks: OpenAPI contract and generated-client drift check
partner offer submission and operator review runtime smoke

business: Marketing owns campaign ticker and partner-offer truth while home discovery owns only the client read projection.
Every commercial mutation retains audit actor correlation and before or after state.
Every stale version is rejected rather than overwritten.
Only client-visible stores and catalog targets may be published.
Client exposure is constrained by publication state and active schedule window.
negative: No partner can submit for a store resolved from request input.
No rejected archived paused expired or out-of-window entity is client visible.
No campaign or offer can skip its governed transition graph.
No hard delete removes marketing audit history.
No DSH marketing component creates WLT ledger balance settlement or payment truth.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-025 code and contract gaps without creating a second home-content owner, mutating WLT truth, deploying production or activating commercial SaaS.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
Marketing remains the canonical owner of campaigns tickers and partner offers.
Home discovery is a read model and measurement ingress only.
No PR merge force push release or production deployment is authorized.
